name: Col du Soudet Loop
description: Experience a difficult 39.8-mile road cycling loop around the Col du Soudet, featuring significant climbs and expansive Pyrenean views.
region_intro: Hiking around Licq-Athérey offers access to the foothills of the Pyrenees, characterized by deep valleys, forested slopes, and mountain passes. The region features a mix of terrain, including river gorges and elevated viewpoints. Trails often traverse varied landscapes, from dense woodlands to open hillsides. Best hiking trails around Licq-Athérey The most popular hiking route is Holzarte footbridge, a difficult 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route leads to the notable Holzarte footbridge, offering views of the surrounding gorges. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of the Col d'Holzarte – Holzarte Footbridge loop from Parking d'Holzarte, a moderate 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This loop provides access to the Col d'Holzarte and the footbridge, with views of the mountain landscape. Local hikers also love the View of the Col d'Holzarte – Holzarte Footbridge loop from Auberge Logibar, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through mountain passes and forested areas, often completed in about 4 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Licq-Athérey is defined by mountain passes, deep valleys, and footbridges over gorges. The network offers options for easy hikes, moderate excursions, and more challenging routes for various ability levels. The routes in Licq-Athérey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Licq-Athérey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Licq-Athérey?
A: There are over 80 hiking routes around Licq-Athérey, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy strolls to challenging mountain treks. The region is known for its deep valleys, forested slopes, and mountain passes in the foothills of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Licq-Athérey?
A: Yes, Licq-Athérey offers 16 easy hiking routes. These trails are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for those new to hiking or families looking for a relaxed outing. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed here, easy trails are often a good starting point.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the trails in Licq-Athérey?
A: Hikers in Licq-Athérey can explore various natural attractions. A prominent feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holzarte Footbridge&lt;/a&gt;, which spans a dramatic gorge. You'll also encounter impressive mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bagargui&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrau Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and can even spot summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Licq-Athérey area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Col d'Holzarte – Holzarte Footbridge loop from Parking d'Holzarte&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option. Another moderate loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Baraltzaga loop from Lichans-Sunhar&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Licq-Athérey?
A: The hiking trails in Licq-Athérey are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars from over 400 reviews. Nearly 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore the region, often praising the varied terrain, from dense woodlands to open hillsides, and the unique experience of crossing footbridges over gorges.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Licq-Athérey?
A: Moderate hikes in Licq-Athérey can vary in duration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Col d'Holzarte – Holzarte Footbridge loop from Parking d'Holzarte&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 1 hour 51 minutes. A longer moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Col d'Holzarte – Holzarte Footbridge loop from Auberge Logibar&lt;/a&gt;, can take about 4 hours 23 minutes.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. Licq-Athérey offers 25 difficult routes for experienced hikers. A notable challenging trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1427242819/holzarte-footbridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holzarte footbridge&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 13.6 km long and typically takes around 5 hours 26 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near the hiking routes?
A: While specific pubs aren't listed, the region does have options for refreshments. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3094689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iraty Chalets&lt;/a&gt; are noted as a restaurant in the area, which could be a good stop after a hike. For accommodation, you might find huts like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabane d'Ardane Gaïnekoa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Licq-Athérey?
A: Given its location in the foothills of the Pyrenees, the best seasons for hiking in Licq-Athérey are generally spring, summer, and early autumn. During these times, the weather is typically more stable, and trails are clear of snow, allowing for full access to the diverse landscapes of valleys, forests, and mountain passes.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options if I want to avoid popular spots?
A: While the Holzarte Footbridge area is popular, with over 80 routes in the region, you can find quieter trails. Exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate or difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20522033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosmendieta loop from Lichans-Sunhar&lt;/a&gt;, might offer a more secluded experience away from the main attractions.

Q: Can I find parking near the trailheads in Licq-Athérey?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads in the region, especially those leading to well-known attractions like the Holzarte Footbridge, typically have designated parking areas. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Col d'Holzarte – Holzarte Footbridge loop from Parking d'Holzarte&lt;/a&gt; explicitly mentions starting from a parking location, indicating availability.
tips: Magnificent road without cars, a pleasure to take it. The landscapes are splendid.

Culminating at 1,540 meters above sea level, the Soudet pass is one of the first to be encountered from the Atlantic coast. The various climbs that lead to it are relatively long and maintain low average percentages despite a few passages around 10%.
Not the easy way to climb to Pierre Saint Martin but the valley is very nice and awsome landscape.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Loop of the Col du Soudet?
faqs.answer: This loop is classified as a "difficult" racebike route, requiring very good fitness. It features significant climbs and challenging gradients, making it best suited for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and road conditions can I expect on this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly well-paved, offering good road surface conditions. However, you will encounter long climbs with varying gradients, including passages around 10% and steeper sections up to 13.7% or 14.5% on certain approaches. While primarily paved, some routes to the Col du Soudet can have unpaved segments, though the main loop generally maintains good surfaces.

faqs.question: What scenic views or points of interest can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect magnificent and varied landscapes. The route Along the Vert d'Arette offers splendid views with little car traffic. As you ascend, you'll pass through sections under tree cover and completely clear areas. The Soudet Pass itself, at 1,540 meters, provides expansive views, and the Col de Soudet Ascent via Sainte-Engrâce is another notable section.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Loop of the Col du Soudet?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, when the mountain passes are clear of snow. Always check local weather forecasts and road conditions before you go, as winter can bring closures due to snow.

faqs.question: Are there any road closures to be aware of, especially in winter?
faqs.answer: Yes, mountain passes in the Pyrénées, including the Col du Soudet, are often subject to closures during winter due to snow and ice. It's crucial to check current road conditions and weather forecasts before planning your ride, especially outside of peak summer months.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the start of this exact loop aren't provided, typical starting points for Col du Soudet routes often include nearby towns like Arette or Sainte-Engrâce, where you can usually find public parking facilities. It's advisable to research parking options in your chosen starting village beforehand.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the Loop of the Col du Soudet. It is a public road accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: Is the Loop of the Col du Soudet dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a challenging road cycling route, it is not typically considered suitable for dogs to accompany you on the bike. If you plan to bring a dog, it would be best to look for alternative, more appropriate activities in the area.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is quite remote, you may find small cafes or restaurants in the villages at the base of the climbs or in nearby towns like Arette or Sainte-Engrâce. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, as options on the pass itself are limited.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this ride?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation changes and potential for varied weather in the mountains, it's recommended to wear appropriate cycling gear, including layers. Bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit, and sun protection. A windbreaker or rain jacket is also advisable, even on sunny days, as conditions can change quickly at higher altitudes.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer:
Yes, the route also passes through sections of

La route des cols des Pyrénées de Larrau à Col d'Aubisque

,

Le Haut Béarn

, and

Le col de La Pierre-Saint-Martin depuis Licq-Atherey

.

waypoints.name: Col de Soudet Ascent via Sainte-Engrâce
